title: Method calls and this

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications draft for Method calls and this; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: ECMAScript Language Specification; living specification accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Method calls and this
sources.claim: JavaScript this is determined by call form, except arrows capture it lexically; extracting a method can lose its receiver.

sources.claim: JavaScript this is determined by call form, except arrows capture it lexically; extracting a method can lose its receiver.

canonicalExplanation.summary: JavaScript this is determined by call form, except arrows capture it lexically; extracting a method can lose its receiver.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Calling user.getName() supplies user as receiver, but assigning the method to a variable removes that call-site receiver. Your goal is to trace and repair this binding across direct, method and callback calls. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Object records and property access; Function declarations, expressions and arrows. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: JavaScript this is determined by call form, except arrows capture it lexically; extracting a method can lose its receiver. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Strict mode, constructors, bind, event callbacks, and modules affect the resulting value. This draft therefore rejects: this is permanently defined where a normal function is written.

includes: Determine this from call form and avoid detached-method and arrow-method surprises.
Trace and repair this binding across direct, method and callback calls.
excludes: Strict mode, constructors, bind, event callbacks, and modules affect the resulting value.

outcomes: Trace and repair this binding across direct, method and callback calls.

examples.title: Method calls and this: concrete case
examples.situation: Calling user.getName() supplies user as receiver, but assigning the method to a variable removes that call-site receiver.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: Calling user.getName() supplies user as receiver, but assigning the method to a variable removes that call-site receiver.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: For normal functions, this comes from the call site; bind it or preserve the receiver when extracting methods.
examples.result: The result follows because For normal functions, this comes from the call site; bind it or preserve the receiver when extracting methods.

examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: For normal functions, this comes from the call site; bind it or preserve the receiver when extracting methods.
